notes: Old skill response also strong; includes ServiceDeps dataclass bonus. Both versions pass all assertions.
Both versions pass all assertions at 100% — the assertions may not be discriminating enough to capture the qualitative improvements in the new skill.
Qualitative analysis shows new skill responses are richer: they include explicit Before/After anti-pattern blocks, Protocol-based abstractions, and test examples that the old skill responses lack.
The new skill's before/after examples in Composition/Inheritance and Pure Functions sections were directly applicable to evals 3 and 1 respectively.
Recommendation: The new skill is meaningfully better in output quality even though pass rates are equal. The before/after examples provide concrete anchors that improve response specificity.
The assertions should be updated to include quality-discriminating checks like 'includes explicit anti-pattern code block' and 'includes test examples' to better capture the new skill's advantage.
